channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Blocked footpath, 25th March
item.description:
The path to Erith station has been blocked off for many weeks causing much inconvenience. What is being done and when will it be finished? I have already contacted Thames Water but have had no reply
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bexley Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shattered glass from a car accident is on the main road and obstructing a driveway. Car debris is on the pavement obstructing the public walking especially for wheelchair users and buggies/ stollers., 25th March
item.description:
Obstruction on the public pavement and main busy road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70, Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large double mattress and other matter flytipped in Chelsfield Road, 25th March
item.description:
Idiots have flytipped a double mattress and other matter next to large tree opposite Rosecroft Close new houses in Chelsfield Road just past mini roundabout on the left many thanks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chelsfield Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on both sides of the pub on the corner of King William Lane and Trafalgar Road, 25th March
item.description:
Great if you can paint over this. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Trafalgar Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 25th March
item.description:
Flytipping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rothschild Street, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Furniture dropped and bits of house cleaning, 25th March
item.description:
The items that were previously put in front of the house at 121 Calvert road for free pick up were subsequently relocated to the space on Woolwich area.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 33 willow rd, 25th March
item.description:
Abandoned bike
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Willow Road, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dreadful potholes down Petts Wood Road, 25th March
item.description:
The holes are so big in the road that cars are swerving to avoid them making it dangerous for oncoming vehicles and cyclists could be badly injured. Chislehurst Rd is being resurfaced yet there are far fewer potholes along this stretch. Petts Wood Road is far worse. The traffic up this road is really heavy with numerous cyclists travelling along it so it is urgent that this work is completed before there is a fatality,
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 64, Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More furniture., 25th March
item.description:
Yet more of the same furniture du owe in the same location and there is a surveillance camera overlooking the area. It’s the same person time and time again

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albion Road, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 25th March
item.description:
Flytipping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Newent Close, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping again, 25th March
item.description:
Endless flytipping...dangerous spike sticking out. Pedestrian walkway which small kids use
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, Elswick Road, Ladywell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flyposting, 25th March
item.description:
Agent is advertising/placing a board on the Bromley Council pavement at entrance to Brookwood Close.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brookwood Close, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Furniture pieces outside flats, 25th March
item.description:
There are pieces of what looks like a wardrobe outside the flats on Green Lane by the bridge. The items have been there many days and I expect dumped by someone who moved out. Please remove as they are dangerous too if they fall on someone. Thanks.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Green Lane, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Single bed and mattress, 25th March
item.description:
Single bed and mattress have been abandoned in Frederick Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Frederick Road, Kennington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Build up of leaves, 25th March
item.description:
Big build up of leaves up the steps and over the footbridge over the railway line. Looks a terrible mess and slippery when wet
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Mead Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loose kerbstone, 25th March
item.description:
One of the kerbstones outside 7 The Chase has worked free of the edge and is lying loose on the roadside. Ideally it needs relocating back into position.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Chase, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Inbetween 45 and 47 marchmont street, 25th March
item.description:
Pavement slab is a trip hazard.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 39, Marchmont Street, St Pancras, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken manhole cover, 25th March
item.description:
One small manhole cover is fully broken with crap filling up in it. People are intentionally throwing garbage into it as well. Another one on the same street has detached from the hole.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Morecambe Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loud church group, 25th March
item.description:
On Saturday from roughly 1pm there was a large religious group from Catford outside black sheep. They were so loud it resulted in us (Robert Dyas) losing trade and have complaints from members of the public. As there was no police about I called the market inspector via shop safe radio to ask them to turn it down. This went on for several hours. How can this noise be reported going forward and are religious groups just allowed to turn up with loud speakers? I can only presume this affected black sheep’s trade as well.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 95, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large fly tip blocking path, 25th March
item.description:
Rubbish all over patth

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Oulton Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
